text.tspan: Number of children with lead levels in blood greater than 10 µg/dL
Children are defined as those aged 0 to 14 years old. There is no defined 'safe' level for lead blood
concentrations. The WHO adopts a threshold of 5 µg/dL as an achievable maximum level in children.
Data source: Ericson et al. (2021). Blood lead levels in low-income and middle-income countries: a systematic review. The Lancet Planetary
